body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Makes some improvements to the longruns based on the results of [this week's runs](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-longruns/builds/1076).

After approval I'll merge this without waiting for CI because it changes only longruns.

## Content
- extends CMIP longruns from 6 months to 1 year of runtime
  - these runs are both stable for at least a year now, so it makes sense to run them for longer
- add an AMIP + prog EDMF + integrated land run, and remove AMIP + prog EDMF + bucket
  - AMIP + prog EDMF is stable at 6 months with the bucket, but fails after ~2 weeks with CMIP + integrated land. I want to add in the AMIP + integrated land case to better understand what causes the failure
- add `implicit_microphysics: false` to 1M jobs

<!---
Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/

- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

----
- [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: longrun improvements

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
We missed this because we add to the fluxes in multiple places (for heat, longwave/shortwave radiation and sensible/latent heat flux are added in two different places; for salinity, precipitation and turbulent moisture flux are added in different places). So we got into to the habit of setting fluxes in one places and adding to them in others, which becomes unclear and hard to keep track of.

This PR explicitly sets the heat and salinity fluxes of the ocean to 0 at the start of a step. Then the fluxes are always added to in `update_sim!` and `update_turbulent_fluxes!`.

## Improvement
A cleaner/more future-proof solution would be to introduce a new method `reset_fluxes!` which sets all fluxes to 0 before we start flux accumulation for a time step. This can be extended for each component model and would be more explicit in terms of what's being done.
title: zero ocean fluxes at start of step
